response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a highly experienced full-stack and web developer, but he is fundamentally mismatched for this role. The most critical hard requirement — native-level Japanese language proficiency — is entirely absent from his profile. Beyond the language barrier, his background is concentrated in TypeScript, Node.js, and web-platform development with no evidence of embedded firmware work, network security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal), Layer-2/Layer-3 networking, or Linux kernel development. His C experience appears limited to a single brief role at National Instruments and a university thesis project. There is no path to bridging these gaps at the mid-senior level this role demands.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience across diverse domains and languages demonstrates strong engineering fundamentals
Consistent Linux usage across multiple roles provides a baseline familiarity with the operating environment
Some low-level C and C++ exposure at National Instruments and in personal projects shows he is not exclusively a high-level language developer
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — this is a non-negotiable hard requirement that immediately disqualifies the candidate
Zero experience with embedded networking security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, SNMP, NETCONF) or network switch hardware
No Linux kernel development, source-code modification, or embedded firmware work in any role throughout his career

key_factors.factor: No Linux kernel or C embedded experience
